manifest.name: System Atlas
manifest.description: Local-first architecture workbench. This pack is the dogfooded model of System Atlas itself.

responsibilities: Maintain intended architecture
Author proposals
Review AI-generated diffs

successCriteria: Atlas stays in sync with repo
AI agents implement migrations without drifting

nodes.name: Architect

responsibilities: Read architecture pack before opening source
Implement proposal diffs
Update architecture files alongside code

metadata.role: Claude Code, Codex, or comparable coding agent

nodes.name: AI Agent

responsibilities: The diagrams must not drift from the code

acceptanceCriteria: Pack Health reports healthy after every commit
Generated files match authored source revision

nodes.name: Living architecture

responsibilities: AI must not silently weaken invariants when implementing a proposal

acceptanceCriteria: Every migration brief lists forbidden changes and acceptance checks
Validation passes after the AI's commit

nodes.name: AI migration safety

responsibilities: Architecture state must live in repo files reviewable in Git

acceptanceCriteria: Architecture pack is human-readable Markdown + YAML

nodes.name: Local-first + Git-friendly

responsibilities: Edit, validate, and export a typed architecture graph
Generate Mermaid, Markdown, context packs, and migration briefs for AI agents
nodes.name: System Atlas

responsibilities: React Flow canvas for each architecture view
Trigger Scan/Validate/Export/Brief via the API
nodes.name: Web Client (Vite + React)

responsibilities: Serve /api endpoints
Read and write the architecture/ pack on disk
Run the workspace scanner
nodes.name: API Server (Express)

responsibilities: Hold the authored architecture state and all derived artifacts
Round-trip cleanly between UI and disk
nodes.name: Architecture Pack (filesystem)

responsibilities: Validation, layout, Mermaid, overview, context-pack, migration-brief, semantic-diff
Pure functions — no I/O
invariants: No filesystem or network imports in this module
nodes.name: atlas-core (pure domain)

responsibilities: React Flow node/edge rendering and selection
nodes.name: Atlas Canvas

responsibilities: Edit selected node/edge/flow properties
nodes.name: Inspector

responsibilities: Typed forms for per-node-type metadata profiles (API contract, schema, runtime, ...)
nodes.name: Structured Editors

responsibilities: Promote scanned files/classes/routes/schemas into authored atlas nodes
nodes.name: Import Review

responsibilities: Browse saved project structure, files, classes, routes, schemas, deps, tests
nodes.name: Code Intelligence Explorer

responsibilities: Read/write architecture/ pack
TS-compiler-based code intelligence scan
Pack-health + revision hashing
nodes.name: atlas-files (I/O + scanner)

responsibilities: Per-machine registry of projects the workbench knows about
Atomic JSON persistence at ~/.system-atlas/workspaces.json (Windows: %APPDATA%/system-atlas/workspaces.json)
Add/select/rename/remove + env-var bootstrap
invariants: Registry path is per-user, never inside a project
nodes.name: workspaces registry

responsibilities: Top-bar dropdown to switch between added projects
Add / rename / remove workspaces inline
Onboarding screen rendered when registry is empty
nodes.name: Workspace Picker + Onboarding

responsibilities: GET /api/project, /api/templates, /api/code-intelligence, /api/pack-health, /api/project/revision
POST /api/draft/validate, /api/export, /api/scan, /api/context-pack, /api/proposal, /api/migration-brief
nodes.name: REST API (/api)

responsibilities: Single-page React app that owns the entire workbench: canvas, inspector, inventory, preview panel

metadata.layout: App shell with sidebar inventory, central canvas, and right-side inspector
metadata.seo: Private/no-index — local dev tool, not deployed

nodes.name: Workbench (/)

responsibilities: UI rendering framework

metadata.rationale: Mature ecosystem, React Flow integration, useState/useMemo are enough for this app's state shape

nodes.name: React 19

responsibilities: Frontend dev server and bundler

metadata.rationale: Fast HMR, simple config, first-class React plugin

nodes.name: Vite 6

responsibilities: Interactive node-and-edge canvas

metadata.rationale: Production-grade graph editor; pan/zoom/select/edge-routing handled out of the box

nodes.name: @xyflow/react

responsibilities: Tiny JSON API server

metadata.rationale: Smallest dependency that gives JSON routes; the API has 11 endpoints with no auth/middleware needs
metadata.reviewCadence: Only if the API grows past ~30 endpoints

nodes.name: Express 5

responsibilities: Type safety across the whole codebase

metadata.rationale: Discriminated unions for NODE_TYPES/EDGE_TYPES/VIEW_IDS make the typed graph self-validating

nodes.name: TypeScript strict mode

responsibilities: Port for the Express API
nodes.name: SYSTEM_ATLAS_API_PORT

responsibilities: Port for the Vite dev server
nodes.name: SYSTEM_ATLAS_WEB_PORT

responsibilities: Absolute path to the project whose architecture/ pack should be read and written
nodes.name: SYSTEM_ATLAS_WORKSPACE

responsibilities: Persist scanned code intelligence between sessions

metadata.consistency: Overwritten as a unit on Export

nodes.name: Evidence files

responsibilities: Tracks all projects the workbench is aware of
Holds currentWorkspaceId so the same browser session can switch between projects without restart
nodes.name: Workspace registry (per-machine)

responsibilities: All derived artifacts (Mermaid, MD, briefs) come from the typed graph, not free-text
nodes.name: The architecture graph is the product core

responsibilities: Architecture state is exported into architecture/ for Git review
nodes.name: Repo files beat an app database for v1

responsibilities: Scanner output never silently rewrites the authored atlas
nodes.name: Observed evidence is separate from intended design

responsibilities: Migration briefs are generated from before/after snapshots, not loose prompts
nodes.name: Proposals are first-class

responsibilities: Each view stores its own positions instead of using a single global node position
nodes.name: Views own their layouts

responsibilities: A single workbench instance manages many projects via an in-UI picker
Per-machine registry persists across restarts; SYSTEM_ATLAS_WORKSPACE remains a one-shot bootstrap
nodes.name: Workspace is runtime state, not env-locked
nodes.notes: Earlier design read SYSTEM_ATLAS_WORKSPACE once at server boot. That forced a restart to switch projects. The runtime registry pattern matches what tools like Postman / TablePlus do — launch once, work on any project.

responsibilities: Vite warns; mermaid and React Flow are the heavy ones
nodes.name: Client bundle creeping past 500 KB

responsibilities: Single file owns validation, layout, generation, diff, import — sustainable for now but watch for further growth

metadata.mitigation: Split along natural seams when next major feature lands

nodes.name: src/lib/atlas.ts past 2000 lines

responsibilities: Without a startup check the API is silently shadowed by whatever already owns the port

metadata.impact: All /api calls 404 against the wrong service
metadata.mitigation: Startup port-conflict check; configurable port via env

nodes.name: Default API port 5174 collides with a-private-project

responsibilities: Export → reload → semantic equality of nodes, edges, flows, views, proposals must hold

metadata.measurement: Validation passes, semanticDiff returns empty after roundtrip

nodes.name: Pack round-trip fidelity

responsibilities: mermaid 11.0–11.14 has open advisories around classDefs / config sanitisation

metadata.mitigation: npm audit fix to the next patched line; render only architect-authored input

nodes.name: Mermaid CSS/HTML injection (CVEs)

edges.description: Vite dev server proxies /api/* to the Express server
exportAtlas writes manifest, concept files, views, generated/, evidence/
All workbench data round-trips through /api/* via src/lib/api.ts

flows.name: Greenfield design → AI implementation
flows.description: Architect models the intended system, creates a proposal between architecture versions, then hands an AI agent a migration brief.

steps.label: Start from blank or generic starter atlas
Add systems, containers, components, datastores, contracts, risks, decisions
Create a proposal capturing the next architecture change
Generate migration brief via /api/migration-brief
AI agent implements the diff and updates architecture files together
Architect reviews, validates, resolves conflicts

failureModes: Proposal acceptance checks are too vague to verify
acceptanceChecks: All flows still have linked tests

flows.name: Brownfield import → first atlas
flows.description: Scan an existing repository, promote useful discovered facts into authored nodes, then commit the architecture pack.

steps.label: Point System Atlas at the consumer project (SYSTEM_ATLAS_WORKSPACE)
Click Scan → /api/scan indexes structure, files, classes, routes, schemas
Review Import Review and promote relevant evidence into authored nodes
Manually model datastores, contracts, flows, risks, decisions
Export the pack so the AI agent reads it on the next session

failureModes: Scanner mis-classifies a file and adds noise to the atlas
Architect over-promotes inferred facts without confirming them
acceptanceChecks: Inferred nodes carry confidence: inferred until reviewed
Pack Health reports healthy after Export

flows.name: UI edit → autosync export
flows.description: Edits in the canvas debounce-export the pack and update Pack Health.

steps.label: Architect edits a node in the Inspector
App debounces, calls /api/export with the current baseRevision
exportAtlas writes the changed concept files and regenerates derived files
Pack Health turns healthy; status footer shows synced timestamp

failureModes: External edit happens between debounce and Export → 409 conflict
Disk write fails mid-export and pack becomes misaligned
acceptanceChecks: 409 surfaces to the user as 'External changes — reload or force'
Pack Health flags misalignment within one revision check

flows.name: Add a project → switch workspace
flows.description: A single workbench instance manages many projects. Adding a workspace registers it and makes it current; switching reloads the pack without restart.

steps.label: User pastes an absolute project path into the picker (or onboarding card)
Client POSTs /api/workspaces { path, name? }
workspaces module validates path, dedupes by canonical form, persists atomically
Server marks the new workspace current; next /api/project resolves against it
Client re-fetches project + health and renders the new pack

failureModes: Concurrent writes from two server instances would race the JSON file (single-user assumption holds)
acceptanceChecks: After switch, Pack Health and inventory reflect the new project within one render

flows.name: External edit reconciliation
flows.description: When architecture files change on disk while the UI has unsaved edits, prompt the architect to reload or force-export.

steps.label: API polls architectureRevision()
Web detects the on-disk revision diverged from the loaded one
User picks Reload (discard UI edits) or Export with force=true

failureModes: User force-exports and loses upstream edits

views.name: Context
views.description: C4-style system boundary, actors, client surfaces, owned systems, teams, and external dependencies.

views.name: Containers
views.description: Apps, services, workers, schedulers, load balancers, contracts, and external dependencies.

views.name: Components
views.description: Services, modules, components, contracts, and their implementation relationships.

views.name: Code
views.description: Files, packages, code symbols, contracts, linked tests, and implementation evidence.

views.name: Classes
views.description: Saved class and interface model from code intelligence, including attributes, methods, inheritance, implementation, and test links.

views.name: Flows
views.description: Critical user and system journeys, traces, failure modes, and acceptance checks.

views.name: API Surface
views.description: API contracts, discovered routes, exposed endpoints, auth concerns, linked tests, and services that implement or depend on them.

views.name: Deployment
views.description: Environments, regions, deployment nodes, replicas, routing, and operational topology.

views.name: Data
views.description: Entities, schemas, stores, queues, caches, ownership, read/write paths, replicas, and retention-sensitive paths.

views.name: Schema Model
views.description: Database schemas, entities, tables, columns, keys, indexes, relations, replicas, migrations, and read/write ownership.

views.name: Domain
views.description: Bounded contexts, domain entities, contracts, events, teams, and model ownership.

views.name: Security
views.description: Trust boundaries, authentication, authorization, threats, mitigations, sensitive data, and controls.

views.name: Concerns
views.description: Stakeholders, concerns, quality drivers, risks, decisions, and the architecture elements that address them.

views.name: Health
views.description: Risks, quality scenarios, reliability concerns, stale architecture, regression exposure, and test gaps.

views.name: Decisions
views.description: ADRs, superseded decisions, tradeoffs, quality scenarios, risks, and rationale links.

views.name: Proposals
views.description: Architecture change proposals, before/after impact, migration briefs, and acceptance checks.
